description: §61.4/§61.9 — an `<endpoint>` with a fully-exhaustive `accepts=` PLUS a DEAD/UNKNOWN arm (`Bogus`, a variant not declared in the `FspMethod` enum) follows the §18.0.1 arm-validity rules: it fires E-MATCH-SUBSET-DEAD-ARM (the §18.0.1 dead-arm diagnostic the §34 E-ENDPOINT-NOT-EXHAUSTIVE row names as the endpoint's reused dead-arm surface), NOT a dedicated E-ENDPOINT-* code. All real variants are covered, so E-ENDPOINT-NOT-EXHAUSTIVE stays silent.
rationale: §61.9: an arm naming a variant not in `accepts=` is the §18.0.1 arm-validity diagnostic (E-MATCH-SUBSET-DEAD-ARM), NOT an E-ENDPOINT-* code — `<endpoint>` reuses that surface. So E-MATCH-SUBSET-DEAD-ARM fires and the whole E-ENDPOINT-* family stays silent. This is the sibling of the duplicate-arm case (E-TYPE-023); both reuse the §18.0.1 arm-validity surface.
